_comment: Common disconnect_reason strings on history.participant records. The exact set varies between Pexip versions — call get_resource_schema on the participant resource (history namespace) to be sure. Use these for the `disconnect_reason=` filter on list_history_participants or for grouping with summarize_calls(group_by='disconnect_reason').

reasons.meaning: Normal hangup. Either side cleanly ended the call.
Specifically the remote participant ended the call.
The conference itself ended while this leg was still connected (e.g. admin called conference/disconnect).
Far end rejected the call attempt (busy, declined, rule deny).
Generic call setup or in-call failure (codec mismatch, network drop).
Disconnect was triggered by an API call (participant/disconnect or conference/disconnect).
Wrong PIN or other credential check failed.
No keep-alive within Pexip's window. Often network problem.
WebRTC ICE negotiation failed — usually a NAT / firewall problem on the client side.
Caller tried to join while the meeting was locked and got dropped from the waiting room.

_query_hints.all_failures: Use list_history_participants with disconnect_reason__in=Call+failed,Call+rejected,Connection+timed+out (Pexip supports Tastypie __in filters).
_query_hints.by_reason_chart: Use summarize_calls(group_by='disconnect_reason', ...) for a single grouped report.
